title: Announcements
content:
Announcements

Okan Homepage

Announcements

Project Number 2025-1-TR01-KA131-HED-00031955 Erasmus+ Student Mobility for Studies

27
						October

2025

Within the scope of the Erasmus+ Key Action 1: Learning Mobility of Individuals (KA131) Program, students will be selected to study abroad during the Fall and Spring semesters of the 2026-2027 Academic Year. It is planned that a total of 22 students will be selected as Main Candidates for Student Study Mobility across the University for the 2025 Contract Period. Associate degree students can only apply for internships. Undergraduate and Graduate students can apply for both study and internship programs. Applications from departments without agreements will not be accepted.

Applications will be made through the

TURNA PORTAL

and will open on

17.11.2025

and close on

01.12.2025

at 23:59. Applications via mail or in-person submissions to the office will not be accepted.

The Erasmus English Written Exam will take place in the classrooms of the Foreign Languages Coordination Office at Tuzla Campus. The classrooms for the written exam will be announced on our website. The oral exam will also be held in the classrooms of the Foreign Languages Coordination Office at Tuzla Campus, and only students who pass the English written exam will be eligible to take the oral exam. Announcements regarding the exam dates and times will be made later on our Erasmus website.

Important Note: The Erasmus+ KA131 Student Study Mobility Program is NOT A FOREIGN LANGUAGE LEARNING PROGRAM. Candidate students are expected to already have sufficient language proficiency to participate in study mobility abroad without issues.

Important Notes:

Two scholarship quotas have been allocated for each      faculty.

However, if no applications are received from a      faculty, the remaining scholarship quota will be transferred to the      faculty with the highest number of applicants. This transfer will be made      based on ranking and score superiority.

Quota numbers are estimated and may vary depending on      the application results and the total scholarship amount provided by the      National Agency.

Students will be funded for one semester in the      2026-2027 Academic Year.

Students who prefer mobility in the Fall semester and      wish to continue studying in the Spring semester should apply considering      that they will not receive a scholarship for the second semester.

Click to here Detailed information about the Inclusion Support provided for Erasmus+ students

All applications will be made online via the Erasmus Portal using e-Government login through

TURNA PORTAL

.

Student Certificate: A barcode document from the      e-Government system or an e-signed document from the OİS system can be      used.

Transcript: Approved updated grade report. Can be      obtained from e-Government system with barcode or e-signed from the OİS      system.

English Exam Result Document: Erasmus English exam      results conducted by our University’s Foreign Languages Coordination      Office (minimum 60/100) will be valid for the application. Students who do      not have a language certificate during the application should tick the box      “I want to take the university foreign language exam”. In addition, scores      obtained from YDS (last 5 years) and YÖKDİL exams accepted by YÖK (minimum      60/100), or equivalent exams recognized by ÖSYM, will be accepted based on      the most recent conversion table published by ÖSYM.

Note: English exams taken during preparatory class or similar preparation      periods are not valid for this application. Students can only use exam      results accepted by our University’s Foreign Languages Coordination Office      or by YÖK/YÖKDİL/ÖSYM.

Official document if you are a child of a martyr/veteran

Official document if you have a      custody/protection/shelter decision

MINIMUM APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS

The student must be enrolled as a full-time student in      any level of formal higher education programs (First Cycle: Undergraduate,      Associate Degree; Second Cycle: Master’s; Third Cycle: Doctorate),

a. First cycle students must have a cumulative GPA of at least 2.20/4.00,      and second and third cycle students must have a GPA of at least 2.50/4.00.

b. For students who have just transitioned from associate degree and whose      transcript is not yet available, the associate degree graduation GPA must      be at least 2.20/4.00.

If the student has previously participated in Erasmus+      higher education mobility activities, the total duration with the new activity      should not exceed 12 months.

Applications must be submitted within the announcement dates opened by our coordination office.

*Students applying for priority points must provide official documents as specified.

EVALUATION PROCESS

Ranking of Erasmus student study mobility candidates      will be based on 50% GPA + 50% language exam result (Erasmus+ score).

10 points will be deducted for each previous mobility      (study or internship).

Results will be evaluated by the Erasmus Coordination Office      appointed by the Rectorate according to relevant criteria. Selection      results will be announced on the Erasmus Coordination Office website.

Disciplinary sanctions or failed courses do not prevent      participation in the Erasmus program.

APPLICATION STEPS

Applications via

TURNA PORTAL

.

Print and sign the online application form (with      photo). Physical submission to the office is not required. The office may      request it if needed.

Updated Student Certificate:

Must be obtained via e-Government with barcode or from       the Student Affairs Office with signature and stamp.

E-signed document from OİS system is acceptable.

Updated Transcript (signed and stamped):

Must be obtained via e-Government with barcode or from       Student Affairs Office with signature and stamp.

E-signed document from OİS system is acceptable.

Transcripts with “sample” label from OİS system are       not valid.

Upload T.C. Identity Card:

Front and back scanned copy in single PDF.

Important Note: Completion of applications and correct document uploads are the responsibility of the applicants. Late applications will not be considered.

Note: All details regarding application steps can be found in the user guide.

TECHNICAL NOTES FOR ONLINE APPLICATION

Upload an acceptable passport-style photo in the profile      picture section.

If declaring a national or international exam score,      uploading the Exam Result Document is mandatory.

For questions:

erasmus@okan.edu.tr

The Erasmus Coordination Office is located at B104, Ground Floor, Faculty of Business and Administration Sciences.

For more information, visit our

Erasmus website

.

TURNA PORTAL

system. Otherwise, your application will be invalid.
